goal: Deliver the Composer + Reviewer sub-agent vertical slice: identity files, per-role contract templates, supporting skills, and the Effect services (ContextEngine, AgentRunner, EvalParser) plus runTask orchestration, testable end-to-end with an echo-agent fake.
constraints: Services are Effect Context.Tag + Layer pairs, swappable in tests.
All new types under src/types/*.d.ts, ambient (no exports), PascalCase filenames.
Tests under __test__/ folders adjacent to the code they exercise.
No `any`, no `as unknown as`. Errors are tagged unions via effect/Schema.

Subagent spawn command is fixed: claude -p --agent <Composer|Reviewer> --output-format stream-json --verbose --dangerously-skip-permissions. Model, tools, skills, maxTurns come from the agent frontmatter.
Never spawn with detached: true — prior incident left zombie claude processes. Use default process-group behavior; cancellation via SIGTERM to the direct child pid.
Subagent frontmatter lists skills explicitly. Do not rely on cwd auto-discovery — sub-agents running as main do not inherit skills.
Templating library: handlebars. Per-invocation render, no compile cache in v0.

Log tee paths: .tap/features/<slug>/logs/<task_id>/iter-NNN-<role>.jsonl (stdout) and .stderr.log (stderr). Eval output .tap/features/<slug>/eval/EVAL_RESULT.md is written by Reviewer itself, not by the harness.

This feature does not deliver a FeatureContract service, a LoopRunner scheduler, a CLI, or any VCS / auto-commit behavior. Caller (future LoopRunner) is responsible for contract I/O, scheduling, retries, and VCS policy.

acceptance.behavioral: bun install exits 0 with handlebars pinned in package.json
bunx tsc --noEmit exits 0 with all new/renamed ambient type files in place
Task, Story, Feature, TaskResult, EvalIssue, RunTaskError, AgentEvent, and the four service-contract types resolve in TypeScript without any import statement
handlebars key appears in the dependencies field of package.json
bun install exits 0 and bun.lock is updated
bunx tsc --noEmit resolves `import Handlebars from 'handlebars'` without error

the TaskStatus type resolves as exactly `"pending" | "in_progress" | "done" | "failed"` with no other variants

Task.id is typed TaskId, Task.depends_on is typed readonly TaskId[], Task.files is typed readonly AbsolutePath[], Story.id is typed StoryId

tasks.title: Extend AgentRunner.d.ts with AgentEvent + run signature

acceptance.behavioral: AgentEvent is a discriminated union with variants system, assistant, user, result each carrying the fields listed in SPECS.md
ContentBlock is a discriminated union with variants text, tool_use, and tool_result

AgentRunOptions type has fields role, stdin, cwd, attempt, logPath, stderrLogPath, evalPath with the types and brands specified: role is 'Composer' | 'Reviewer', path fields use AbsolutePath brand from Contract.d.ts
bunx tsc --noEmit exits 0
EvalIssue type has fields acceptanceFailed: string, file: AbsolutePath, problem: string, suggestedFix: string
TaskResult type has fields taskId: TaskId, attempt: number, verdict, rationale, issues, composerLogPath: AbsolutePath, reviewerLogPath: AbsolutePath, evalResultPath: AbsolutePath, durationMs: number

RunTaskError is a discriminated union with exactly six variants: AgentSpawnFailed (carries role), AgentMaxTurnsExceeded (carries role), EvalResultMissing, EvalParseFailed, TemplateRenderFailed, FilesystemError, each with the specified fields; path fields in EvalResultMissing and FilesystemError use the AbsolutePath brand

stories.title: Static content: sub-agent identities, skills, prompt templates

acceptance.behavioral: .claude/agents/Composer.md and .claude/agents/Reviewer.md exist on disk with valid YAML frontmatter and body matching the agreed shape
.claude/skills/tdd/SKILL.md, .claude/skills/anti-patterns/SKILL.md, and .claude/skills/code-review/SKILL.md all exist on disk with the agreed bodies
.tap/prompts/COMPOSER_CONTRACT.md and .tap/prompts/REVIEWER_CONTRACT.md exist with handlebars placeholders matching the RenderInput shape
YAML frontmatter parses without error and Handlebars.compile succeeds on each template file
.claude/agents/Composer.md YAML frontmatter contains name: Composer, model: sonnet, skills: [tdd, anti-patterns], maxTurns: 50, and a single-sentence description
Composer.md body text contains rules stating: edit only task.files, no commits/pushes, run bun test and bunx tsc --noEmit before exit, read prior_eval_path on retry
YAML frontmatter in Composer.md parses without error using a standard YAML parser
.claude/agents/Reviewer.md YAML frontmatter contains name: Reviewer, model: opus, skills: [anti-patterns, code-review], maxTurns: 50, and a single-sentence description
Reviewer.md body text states: do not edit source code, do not commit, run bun test and bunx tsc --noEmit independently, write output only to EVAL_RESULT.md at eval_path
YAML frontmatter in Reviewer.md parses without error using a standard YAML parser
.claude/skills/tdd/SKILL.md frontmatter description field explicitly mentions the trigger condition of acceptance referring to a test file
tdd SKILL.md body text contains the red-green-refactor loop, one-assertion-per-test rule, behavior-named tests, and small-fast-unit preference
tdd SKILL.md body text does not contain Bun-, TypeScript-, or Effect-specific rules
.claude/skills/tdd/SKILL.md exists at the path recognized by the Claude Code harness skill registry
.claude/skills/anti-patterns/SKILL.md frontmatter description mentions writing or reviewing code before finalizing as the trigger

anti-patterns SKILL.md body text enumerates monolithic files (>~300 lines), 3+ duplication rule, no impurity in pure zones, deep-nesting smell, magic values, vague names, commented-out code, and implicit contracts
anti-patterns SKILL.md body text does not contain language- or framework-specific rules
.claude/skills/anti-patterns/SKILL.md exists at the path recognized by the Claude Code harness skill registry
.claude/skills/code-review/SKILL.md frontmatter description identifies the trigger as acting as Reviewer in the tap-tool Ralph loop

code-review SKILL.md body text covers per-criterion classification (Satisfied / Not satisfied / Partial), zero-trust verification steps, scope-check (nothing touched outside task.files), and verdict rules (PASS only when every criterion satisfied, tests green, no anti-pattern violations)
code-review SKILL.md body text contains a reference to the <eval:verdict> schema and mentions REVIEWER_CONTRACT.md for emission details
.claude/skills/code-review/SKILL.md exists at the path recognized by the Claude Code harness skill registry

COMPOSER_CONTRACT.md template file contains handlebars placeholders for feature_goal, feature_constraints, task_id, task_title, task_files, task_acceptance, specs_path, contract_path, prior_eval_path, and git_status
COMPOSER_CONTRACT.md template ends with an explicit kickoff directive such as `## Begin now.`

COMPOSER_CONTRACT.md template uses triple-brace `{{{ }}}` for feature_goal, feature_constraints items, task_title, task_files items, task_acceptance items, git_status and double-brace `{{ }}` for task_id, specs_path, contract_path
COMPOSER_CONTRACT.md template contains an `{{#if prior_eval_path}}...{{/if}}` block that wraps the prior_eval_path placeholder so it is omitted on attempt 1
Handlebars.compile called on the COMPOSER_CONTRACT.md file contents returns without throwing
REVIEWER_CONTRACT.md template file contains handlebars placeholders for task_id, task_title, task_files, task_acceptance, specs_path, contract_path, and eval_path
REVIEWER_CONTRACT.md template contains an instruction to write EVAL_RESULT.md at {{eval_path}} using the Write tool, with the verdict/rationale/issues block format shown inline
REVIEWER_CONTRACT.md template uses triple-brace {{{ }}} for task_title, task_files items, task_acceptance items and double-brace {{ }} for controlled IDs and paths
REVIEWER_CONTRACT.md template ends with an explicit kickoff directive
Handlebars.compile called on REVIEWER_CONTRACT.md file contents returns without throwing
ContextEngine is exported as an Effect Context.Tag with a ContextEngineLive Layer that reads templates and renders Composer and Reviewer contracts using handlebars
calling renderComposer or renderReviewer with an undefined placeholder value returns an Effect that fails with { _tag: 'TemplateRenderFailed' } rather than succeeding with a blank placeholder

src/services/ContextEngine.ts exports a ContextEngine Context.Tag with renderComposer and renderReviewer methods matching the signatures renderComposer(input: ComposerRenderInput): Effect<string, TemplateRenderError> and renderReviewer(input: ReviewerRenderInput): Effect<string, TemplateRenderError>
acceptance.mechanism: ContextEngine Context.Tag, renderComposer(input: ComposerRenderInput): Effect<string, TemplateRenderError>, renderReviewer(input: ReviewerRenderInput): Effect<string, TemplateRenderError>

acceptance.behavioral: src/services/ContextEngine.ts exports a makeContextEngine function that accepts two template strings and returns a ContextEngine service instance without reading disk
ContextEngineLive is a Layer that reads .tap/prompts/COMPOSER_CONTRACT.md and .tap/prompts/REVIEWER_CONTRACT.md from disk via @effect/platform FileSystem and delegates to makeContextEngine

rendering a template with an undefined placeholder fails the Effect with a TemplateRenderError containing a missingKey field extracted from the Handlebars error message matching the pattern `"<name>" not defined`
acceptance.mechanism: handlebars strict: true, TemplateRenderError with missingKey

acceptance.behavioral: the rendered template context uses snake_case keys (feature_goal, feature_constraints, etc.) and priorEval Option is coerced to empty string when absent with no null/undefined in the context

EvalParser extracts <eval:verdict>, <eval:rationale>, <eval:issues> blocks via regex non-greedy match and parses issues YAML into EvalIssue[] using the yaml npm lib (v2.8.3), mapping acceptance_failed to acceptanceFailed, suggested_fix to suggestedFix, and branding file as AbsolutePath at the parse boundary

AgentRunnerLive spawns the claude command with flags `--output-format stream-json --verbose --dangerously-skip-permissions`, tees stdout to the jsonl log, tees stderr to the stderr log, and returns events with the final result event
AgentRunnerEcho is a Layer that returns scripted AgentEvent arrays and writes canned evalFileContent to evalPath when role is Reviewer
neither AgentRunnerLive nor AgentRunnerEcho passes detached: true to the process spawn call
AgentRunner.ts uses @effect/platform Command and CommandExecutor from @effect/platform-bun to spawn the claude command with stdin from AgentRunOptions.stdin
AgentRunner.ts parses stdout NDJSON line by line using AgentEventSchema exported from the file via decodeAgentEventLine, and tees raw lines to AgentRunOptions.logPath
AgentRunner.ts writes stderr output to AgentRunOptions.stderrLogPath
AgentRunner.ts does not pass detached: true and sends SIGTERM to the child process on Effect interruption via the Command API

src/services/AgentRunner.ts exports an AgentRunner Context.Tag with run method signature run(opts: AgentRunOptions): Effect<{ events: ReadonlyArray<AgentEvent>; result: Extract<AgentEvent, { type: 'result' }> }, AgentSpawnFailed | AgentMaxTurnsExceeded | FilesystemError, FileSystem.FileSystem>
acceptance.mechanism: AgentRunner Context.Tag, run(opts: AgentRunOptions): Effect<{ events: ReadonlyArray<AgentEvent>; result: Extract<AgentEvent, { type: 'result' }> }, AgentSpawnFailed | AgentMaxTurnsExceeded | FilesystemError, FileSystem.FileSystem>

acceptance.behavioral: AgentRunner.ts maps result.subtype === 'error_max_turns' to AgentMaxTurnsExceeded, non-zero exit to AgentSpawnFailed with role and captured stderr, and FileSystem errors during log tee to FilesystemError with branded path

AgentRunnerEcho writes evalFileContent to evalPath via FileSystem when role is Reviewer and evalFileContent is present
AgentRunnerEcho writes scripted events as JSONL to logPath so the log file exists on disk with the expected content
AgentRunnerEcho returns AgentMaxTurnsExceeded or AgentSpawnFailed when script exit is maxTurns or spawnFail
AgentRunnerEcho.run does not spawn any OS subprocess

src/services/RunTask.ts exports runTask with the Effect signature runTask(task: Task, feature: Feature, paths: RunTaskPaths): Effect<TaskResult, RunTaskError, AgentRunner | ContextEngine | EvalParser | FileSystem> and RunTaskPaths with AbsolutePath fields featureRoot, specsPath, contractPath
acceptance.mechanism: runTask(task: Task, feature: Feature, paths: RunTaskPaths): Effect<TaskResult, RunTaskError, AgentRunner | ContextEngine | EvalParser | FileSystem>

acceptance.behavioral: runTask executes steps in order: compute log/eval paths from paths and attempt (iter-NNN zero-padded); Clock.currentTimeMillis for start; fs.makeDirectory({ recursive: true }) for logs and eval dirs; render Composer contract; invoke AgentRunner for Composer; render Reviewer contract with evalPath; invoke AgentRunner for Reviewer; fs.exists check on eval path; parse with EvalParser; Clock end; assemble TaskResult with branded paths and durationMs
acceptance.mechanism: Clock.currentTimeMillis, fs.makeDirectory({ recursive: true }), Option.none(), fs.exists, durationMs

acceptance.behavioral: runTask never surfaces raw Effect errors; all FS boundary errors from makeDirectory, readFileString, and exists are mapped to FilesystemError with branded path
RunTask.ts imports filesystemError from ./AgentRunner and that constructor is re-exported from src/services/index.ts
RunTask.ts contains no any or as unknown as; a single brand<B>(s) helper with one as cast brands six path outputs
